Figure 6: Running time comparison across imputation methods on a representative synthetic benchmark with moderate complexity settings (n = 5000, p = 200).

Figure 1: Overview of MissBGM. The model jointly represents a data-generating process pθ(X | Z) and a missingness mechanism pϕ(R | X). During inference, observed entries remain fixed while latent variables and missing values are updated alternately. Per-sample posterior inference then provides both point estimates and uncertainty quantification for missing values.
Figure 4: RMSE with different missing rate on the synthetic benchmark with n = 1000 and p = 50.
Figure 3: RMSE with different missing rate on the synthetic benchmark with n = 500 and p = 50.
Figure 2: Robustness experiments on synthetic benchmark. (a) RMSE with varying dimensionality p at n = 5000 and missing rate 20%. (b) RMSE with varying missing rates at n = 5000 and p = 50.
